response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and web engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's mandatory requirements. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project—nowhere near the five to ten years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. He has no demonstrated Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge; his RIDE Capital stint involved retail investment tooling in Node.js, not capital-markets systems. SQL appears peripherally in his skill list but is not a focus of his work, and there is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle usage. The optional desirable skills (Summit, STL, Kafka) are also absent.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building low-latency, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby using async I/O and performance-oriented techniques
Solid CI/CD and DevOps practice across multiple roles (GitLab CI, Docker, Kubernetes, trunk-based development)
Brief but real exposure to C/C++ at National Instruments, establishing baseline familiarity with the language
concerns: C++ experience is approximately six months total versus the five to ten years mandated—the single most critical gap
No Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Investment Banking, or Capital Markets domain knowledge anywhere in the profile
No evidence of Windows-platform development; career is Linux/web-centric with no SQL Server or Oracle usage
